policy: Only cited external benchmarks or verbatim standard closed-form formulas may be called ground truth.

registry.ground_truth_policy: authoritative for unit-disk DtN modal rows; raw finite-cycle Q rows remain diagnostics
registry.reference_quantity: Steklov eigenvalues 0,1,1,2,2,... with harmonic traces r^k cos(k theta), r^k sin(k theta)
registry.repo_status: implemented in the repaid disk DtN rows and exact modal PDE calibration
registry.source: Girouard and Polterovich, Spectral geometry of the Steklov problem, 2017 survey

registry.ground_truth_policy: authoritative after the NIST formula and parameters are implemented verbatim
registry.reference_quantity: exact polynomial solution and derived forcing/boundary data
registry.repo_status: registered; should be ported into the Q/FEM comparison before any square-domain accuracy claim
registry.source: William F. Mitchell, A Collection of 2D Elliptic Problems for Testing Adaptive Algorithms, NISTIR 7668, 2010

registry.ground_truth_policy: authoritative only for the externally specified cases, not for lookalike local variants
registry.problem: 2D elliptic problems with singularities and near singularities
registry.reference_quantity: published exact solutions or stated benchmark data
registry.repo_status: registered; replace ad-hoc funky manufactured cases when exact NIST formulas are ported
registry.source: William F. Mitchell, A Collection of 2D Elliptic Problems for Testing Adaptive Algorithms, NISTIR 7668, 2010

registry.domain: Motz rectangle with boundary-condition change at the singular point
registry.ground_truth_policy: the local Motz-like case is not ground truth; use the published Motz data verbatim
registry.problem: Motz problem, Laplace equation with mixed boundary-condition corner singularity
registry.reference_quantity: Rosser-Papamichael analytical solution coefficients as cited in the benchmark literature
registry.repo_status: diagnostic Motz-like corner rows exist; not yet accepted as ground truth until exact Motz geometry and coefficients are ported
registry.source: Georgiou, Olson, Smyrlis, A singular function boundary integral method for the Laplace equation, Communications in Numerical Methods in Engineering 12, 1996

registry.ground_truth_policy: authoritative for eigenvalue comparisons once the exact L-shape sampling and eigen-solver are wired
registry.problem: Dirichlet Laplacian eigenvalue on the L-shaped domain
registry.repo_status: registered; current corner flux rows are not this eigenvalue benchmark
registry.source: Betcke and Trefethen, Reviving the method of particular solutions, SIAM Review 47, 2005

registry.ground_truth_policy: the held-out fact is Dirichlet isospectral equality; Q separation is a different operator-level observable
registry.reference_quantity: Dirichlet Laplacian spectra are identical by transplantation; exact numeric eigenvalues are nontrivial and not the ground-truth claim
registry.repo_status: Q-spectrum separation experiment exists; must be described as a Q-operator diagnostic, not a contradiction of Dirichlet isospectrality
registry.source: Gordon, Webb, Wolpert, One cannot hear the shape of a drum, Inventiones Mathematicae 110, 1992; Driscoll isospectral-drums notes

registry.domain: smooth and corner curves used in QBX accuracy tests
registry.external_status: standard near-boundary layer-potential quadrature benchmark family
registry.ground_truth_policy: QBX is a competitor method; reference must be external analytic/overresolved data, not QBX output itself
registry.problem: singular and nearly singular layer-potential evaluation
registry.reference_quantity: overresolved or analytic layer-potential values for the stated QBX test cases
registry.repo_status: QBX comparison rows exist; accepted ground truth requires matching the published geometry, density, target protocol, and reference resolution
registry.source: Klöckner, Barnett, Greengard, O'Neil, Quadrature by Expansion, Journal of Computational Physics 252, 2013

registry.ground_truth_policy: authoritative for circular modal cases and useful as a calibration gate, but not evidence for arbitrary domains
registry.reference_quantity: Bessel/Hankel modal ratios such as k J'_m(k)/J_m(k) and k H_m'(k)/H_m(k)
registry.repo_status: implemented in the disk Helmholtz/DtN scripts; should be labeled as closed-form standard reference, not FEM truth
registry.source: NIST Digital Library of Mathematical Functions, Chapter 10 Bessel Functions

registry.ground_truth_policy: use as a protocol/source benchmark for corner correction, not as an unlabeled local proxy
registry.problem: elliptic boundary integral equations with corner singularities
registry.reference_quantity: published corner singular quadrature and compressed inverse-preconditioning results
registry.repo_status: registered; current corner-fix demos need exact protocol alignment before becoming ground truth rows
registry.source: Helsing and Ojala, Corner singularities for elliptic problems, Journal of Computational Physics 227, 2008
